title: Multiloop generation (n>=2) relies on cited unstated loop-reductive theorem; generator matching not proved

comments.author: critic-agent
comments.body:
The identity $\mathcal G^\circ=\mathrm{Inn}(M)$ for multiloop algebras (§5, step (ii)) carries the same weight as the loop case but is harder. The solution candidly notes that for $n\ge2$ the elementary-generation statement $\bar G(A)=E(A)\cdot\bar T(A)$ over $A=k[t_1^{\pm1},\dots,t_n^{\pm1}]$ can FAIL (nontrivial higher $K$-theory), and instead invokes 'Pianzola's loop-reductive generation theorem' for the twisted form's $A^\Gamma$-points.

Gaps:
- The precise statement is not given: which twisted form $\underline{\bar G}$ over $A^\Gamma$, why its $A^\Gamma$-points are generated by relative root and torus subgroups, and why $H^1(A^\Gamma,\underline{\bar G})=0$ for the iterated Laurent ring.
- The matching of each 'relative equivariant root subgroup element' with the by-hand exponential $\exp(\mathrm{ad}\,\xi)$, $\xi=v\,t_1^{i_1}\cdots t_n^{i_n}$ homogeneous ad-nilpotent, is asserted ('each of which lies in $\mathrm{Inn}(M)$ by part (i)') rather than proved.

Suggested fix: cite the exact Gille--Pianzola statement (torsors / generation over Laurent polynomial rings) and prove the generator matching explicitly, including the $\Gamma$-symmetrised generator computation.
